ALL PROJECTS


Projects

Production-grade systems, ML infrastructure, and full-stack applications — built at USC and beyond.



SYSTEMS & HIGH-PERFORMANCE COMPUTING




C++ · CUDA · 2025


High-Performance Parallel Computing System


Implemented CUDA kernels for matrix operations; applied shared-memory tiling to minimize global memory latency. Achieved 4× speedup over CPU baseline by optimizing warp divergence, memory coalescing, and occupancy via profiling. Evaluated compute-to-memory ratio to identify and resolve architectural bottlenecks in parallel distribution.


C++ CUDA GPU Profiling Parallel Algorithms Memory Optimization




Spark · Hadoop · 2025


Distributed Data Processing Framework


Built distributed Spark workflows (RDD/DataFrame) on multi-million row datasets; optimized joins and partitioning strategies. Reduced job runtime by 30% via shuffle cost analysis; compared SQL/NoSQL performance under heavy workloads. Evaluated scalability and query consistency across increasing data volumes.


Spark Hadoop RDD DataFrame SQL NoSQL




MACHINE LEARNING & DATA SCIENCE




Python · Scikit-learn · 2025


Machine Learning Pipeline Infrastructure


Designed modular ML workflow covering ingestion, training, and evaluation; implemented regularized models with stratified cross-validation. Improved F1-score by 12% via feature selection and recursive elimination. Conducted bias-variance diagnostics and evaluated ROC/AUC stability across model variants.


Python Scikit-learn PyTorch Feature Engineering Cross-Validation




Python · 2025


Custom SQL/NoSQL Query Engine


Developed extensible query engine supporting filter, join, and aggregation operations with modular CSV/JSON parsers. Implemented chunk-based loading architecture reducing peak memory usage by 70% on 1M+ row datasets. Designed modular execution pipeline enabling scalable backend expansion.


Python SQL NoSQL Memory Optimization




FULL-STACK & APPLIED AI




Flask · BLE · 2024


Beacon-Based Campus Navigation System


Developed Flask backend and routing algorithms for a BLE-based indoor navigation system serving 200+ students. Reduced location query latency from seconds to milliseconds via database optimization. Designed scalable API endpoints for real-time location queries; deployed for live campus usage.


Flask BLE REST API Python Database




AI Systems · Champion · 2024


AI-Driven Campus Smart Innovation System


Led 48-hour agile development of an AI-driven campus system as part of the Champion team at the 2024 AI Era Campus Smart Innovation Hackathon. Architected and integrated ML components; built working prototype and secured 1st Place by presenting technical design and business value to judges.


AI/ML System Design Hackathon 🏆 Champion

title: HsiangYu (Eric) Tsai
text:
ABOUT






MS Applied Data Science @ USC  ·  Los Angeles, CA


basstsai2002@gmail.com  ·  GitHub  ·  LinkedIn





Who I Am


I’m a data scientist and systems engineer currently pursuing my Master of Applied Data Science at the University of Southern California. I grew up in Taipei, where I completed my undergraduate degree in Data Science at Soochow University, and moved to Los Angeles in 2025 to continue building at the intersection of high-performance computing and machine learning.


My work sits at the edge of data science and systems engineering — I care deeply about performance, scalability, and the gap between a working proof-of-concept and something that holds up in production. Whether it’s writing CUDA kernels, tuning Spark jobs, or designing modular ML pipelines, I try to understand not just what works, but why it works at scale.




What I’m Working On


At USC, I’m focused on distributed data systems, GPU acceleration, scalable ML pipelines, and performance-aware computation. I’m actively building toward data engineering and ML systems roles, and I’m open to Summer 2026 internship and research opportunities where I can contribute to real infrastructure challenges.


Outside of coursework, I’m drawn to projects where careful system design creates measurable impact — the kind of work where a 4× speedup or a 70% memory reduction isn’t an accident, but the result of deeply understanding how hardware and software interact.




Technical Interests



High-Performance Computing CUDA, GPU kernels, memory coalescing, warp optimization, parallel algorithms


Distributed Systems Apache Spark, Hadoop, RDD/DataFrame, shuffle optimization, partitioning


Machine Learning Pipeline design, model evaluation, feature engineering, bias-variance analysis


Systems & Infra Python, C/C++, Docker, REST APIs, Linux, Git, Flask





Beyond the Code


When I’m not thinking about data pipelines, I enjoy photography — the cover image on this site was taken on my Fujifilm. I’m interested in the craft of visual storytelling, which in some ways isn’t so different from data visualization: both are about finding the most honest and clear way to show something true.


I’m also drawn to cities and the way they work. Moving from Taipei to Los Angeles has given me a new appreciation for how infrastructure — physical and digital — shapes daily life in ways most people never notice.



View My Projects View CV
